title: Regulatory Affairs
description:
NTWK, azienda italiana operante nella ricerca e selezione del personale, si occupa di soddisfare le necessità dei clienti offrendo servizi personalizzati.

  

  

**Posizione:**
--------------

Per azienda cliente operante nel settore della produzione di film termoplastici, si ricerca un/una **Regulatory Affairs** con forte approccio operativo, che gestirà direttamente le attività regolatorie relative a prodotti in PVC destinati ad applicazioni food contact, medicali e industriali, con particolare attenzione ai temi di sostenibilità, SVHC e alternative ai plastificanti tradizionali.

 **Responsabilità principali**

* Definizione e implementazione della strategia regolatoria per prodotti in PVC
* Gestione operativa della conformità normativa dei prodotti in PVC
* Redazione, aggiornamento e gestione di dichiarazioni di conformità e documentazione tecnica
* Verifica della conformità di materie prime, additivi e formulazioni secondo le normative europee e internazionali e a seconda delle applicazioni:REACH e CLP (incluse SVHC e Candidate List)RoHS/MOCA / Food ContactApplicazioni medicali (supporto regolatorio sui materiali)
* Valutazione dell’impatto regolatorio e tecnico di SVHC, restrizioni e autorizzazioni
* Supporto a progetti di sostituzione di sostanze critiche e introduzione di plastificanti alternativi
* Collaborazione con R\&D per la valutazione regolatoria di nuove formulazioni e materie prime esistenti e più sostenibili
* Gestione della documentazione regolatoria dei fornitori e assessment di conformità
* Supporto operativo a R\&D, Qualità, Produzione, Acquisti e Commerciale
* Gestione delle richieste regolatorie dei clienti (questionari, tender, dichiarazioni di sostenibilità)
* Supporto ad audit clienti, ispezioni e qualifiche
* Monitoraggio continuo degli aggiornamenti normativi e loro applicazione pratica sui prodotti esistenti

**Requisiti:**
--------------

### **Requisiti**

* Laurea in discipline scientifiche (Chimica, Chimica Industriale, Ingegneria dei Materiali o affini)
* Esperienza operativa in Regulatory Affairs nel settore PVC / plastica / materiali
* Conoscenza pratica di:
* REACH, SVHC, restrizioni e Candidate List
* Normative food contact e medicale applicabili ai materiali
* Conoscenza approfondita delle normative chimiche e di prodotto
* Esperienza nella gestione documentale e nel supporto a progetti di riformulazione
* Capacità di gestione autonoma di tematiche regolatorie complesse
* Precisione, autonomia e approccio pratico alla risoluzione dei problemi
* Supporto a audit clienti
* Ottime capacità analitiche, organizzative e comunicative
* Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese

 **Costituiscono un plus:**

* Esperienza con plastificanti alternativi e strategie di sostituzione
* Esperienza in audit e customer assessment

**Altre informazioni:**
-----------------------

### **Cosa si offre:**

* Ruolo operativo con responsabilità diretta sui prodotti
* Coinvolgimento attivo in progetti di innovazione e sostenibilità
* Pacchetto retributivo commisurato all’esperienza

 **Sede di lavoro:** Gorla Minore (VA)

title: Quality & Regulatory Specialist
description:
**Company Description** **At Dr Reddy's "Good Health Can't Wait"**  

Dr. Reddy’s in Milan, Italy is part of an established 40year pharma company with global success. This is an exciting time to join an established, enthusiastic, experienced, innovative, growing team with a robust pipeline and expected to deliver multiple successful commercial product launches in the next 5 years. Passionate about people and transformative medicines? Deeply motivated by delivering good health. **Come build our team together!**

**Diversity, Equity \& Inclusion**  

At Dr Reddy’s, we are deeply committed to building a diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace where everyone belongs and is valued for their contributions to the team. We are most interested in finding the best candidate for the role and are open to exploring candidates with a less traditional background.

 **Job Description**  

Dr.Reddy's is seeking a dynamic **QA \& RA Specialist** to bebased on site and reporting to QA \& RA Manager.

**Key Responsibilities:**

**Regulatory Affairs**

* **Support regulatory submissions** for new registrations, variations, renewals, transfers, and line extensions,
* **Prepare and review regulatory documentation**, including Module 1 updates, local annexes and product information (SPC, PIL, labelling),
* **Manage artwork processes**: review printed materials (PIL, labelling, cartons), ensure regulatory compliance, and upload data into the artwork management tool,
* **Monitor regulatory changes** (AIFA guidelines, European Commission, EMA updates),
* **Support approvals of variations at local level**, including updating on AIFA portal where needed,
* **Maintain local regulatory databases** and ensure documentation accuracy for inspections
* **Support lifecycle management** activities for marketed products

**Quality Assurance**

* **Participate in drafting, updating and implementing QA procedures** (SOPs, work instructions, forms) in alignment with global and GDP/GMP requirements
* **Support batch release activities**, including collecting and archiving batch documentation
* **Support product quality complaints**, including collection of information/documentation for initial assessment
* **Support supplier and service provider qualification**, including documentation collection and audit preparation
* **Support Annual Product Quality Review (PQR) and Annual Quality Review (AQR)** preparation and follow\-up
* **Support temperature excursion assessment**
* **Manage clearance (batch release) and market distribution documentation**, ensuring timely availability for commercial teams

**Compliance and Documentation Management**

* **Maintain controlled documents** (SOPs, forms, templates) within the Quality Management System (QMS)
* **Ensure training compliance**, including tracking and supporting training activities for local teams

**Affiliate Cross Functional Support**

* **Support tender documentation**, including providing regulatory certificates, declarations, or quality statements
* **Collaborate with supply chain** on product availability, artwork changes, and regulatory impact of stock management
* **Facilitate the information flow** among local warehouse provider and HQ
* **Support to the implementation of the serialization project**
* **Participate in global/regional QA/RA initiatives** and working groups

 **Qualifications** **Educational qualification:** Degree in Scientific area (Biology / Biotechnologies / Biomedical Engineering)

  

**Minimum work experience**:

* 1\-3 years of experience in pharma companies

**Skills \& attributes:**

* Excellent Stakeholder management
* Solution focused, ability to solve problems in timely manner
* Ability to adapt to dynamic environment
* Good cross functional collaboration and team work

 **Additional Information** **Benefits Offered**  

At Dr Reddy’s we actively help to catalyse your career growth and professional development through personalised learning programs.  

The benefits you will enjoy at Dr Reddy’s are on par with the best industry standards.

  

**Our Work Culture**  

Ask any employee at Dr Reddy’s why they come to work every day and they’ll say, because Good Health Can’t Wait. This is our credo as well as the guiding principle behind all our actions. We see healthcare solutions not only as scientific formulations, but as a means to help patients lead healthier lives, and we’re always attuned to the new and the next to empower people to stay fit. And to do this, we foster a culture of empathy and dynamism. People are at the core of our journey over the last few decades. They have been supported by an enabling environment that buoys individual ability while fostering teamwork and shared success. We believe that when people with diverse skills are bound together by a common purpose and value system, they can make magic.

title: Stagista QA/RA Transportation
description:
**Before you apply to a job, select your language preference from the options available at the top right of this page.**


Explore your next opportunity at a Fortune Global 500 organization. Envision innovative possibilities, experience our rewarding culture, and work with talented teams that help you become better every day. We know what it takes to lead UPS into tomorrow—people with a unique combination of skill \+ passion. If you have the qualities and drive to lead yourself or teams, there are roles ready to cultivate your skills and take you to the next level.

**Job Description:**


Sede: Pozzuolo Martesana


Durata: 6 mesi con possibilita’ di rinnovo


La risorsa collaborerà con diversi livelli di management su progetti che contribuiscono al successo del team. Utilizzerà conoscenze, competenze e capacità specifiche della funzione per supportare attività progettuali, presentazioni e iniziative di miglioramento continuo. Potranno inoltre essere assegnate ulteriori attività coerenti con il percorso formativo.

**Descrizione del ruolo:**  

Siamo alla ricerca di uno/a stagista QA/RA motivato/a e orientato/a al dettaglio, da inserire nel team Quality Assurance e Regulatory Affairs nell’ambito del trasporto. Il ruolo offre l’opportunità di acquisire esperienza pratica nei sistemi di gestione qualità, nelle attività di compliance e nei processi operativi in un contesto dinamico legato alla logistica e al trasporto.

**Principali responsabilità:**

* Supportare la revisione e la gestione della documentazione relativa alla calibrazione dei trailer e alla conformità dei trasporti.
* Collaborare alla raccolta e archiviazione della documentazione relativa alla formazione.
* Contribuire all’armonizzazione e aggiornamento delle Procedure Operative Standard (SOP) in linea con i requisiti aziendali e normativi.
* Supportare la preparazione di audit interni ed esterni (es. ISO 9001,GDP e audit clienti).
* Affiancare il team nella gestione delle deviazioni, nelle indagini e nel monitoraggio delle azioni correttive e preventive (CAPA).
* Partecipare alla raccolta dati qualità, gestione reclami e attività di reporting.
* Supportare le attività di formazione interna su standard e normative (GMP, GDP, ISO, ecc.)
* Collaborare ai processi di qualifica fornitori

**Profilo del candidato:**

* Laurea in ambito scientifico (biologia, biotecnologie farmacia, CTF, chimica o affini)
* Predisposizione ad attività di precisione e di ufficio; buone capacità organizzative e attenzione al dettaglio
* Spiccato interesse per l’area QA, Affari Regolatori in ambito Logistica e Trasporti.
* Lingua inglese fluente (da livello B2\)
* Buone capacità comunicative e predisposizione al lavoro in team
* Proattività e forte motivazione all’apprendimento in contesti dinamici

**Cosa offriamo:**

* Rimborso spese di 600 €
* Buoni pasto: 5 €
* Lavoro su giornata (lunedì/venerdi)
* Possibilita’ di smartworking (massimo 1 giorno a settimana dopo i primi 6 mesi di affiancamento)

 **Employee Type:**

  

Intern  

UPS is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination, harassment, and retaliation.

title: REGULATORY AFFAIRS SPECIALIST
description:
**Chi cerchiamo**

Per la nostra sede di Milano \- San Babila siamo alla ricerca di una risorsa da inserire all'interno della struttura **REGOLAZIONE PRODUZIONE E MERCATI ELETTRICO, GAS E IDROGENO.** La struttura ha lo scopo di presidiare la normativa di riferimento in materia regolatoria per gli ambiti di competenza, supportando il business nell'interpretazione della stessa e nella gestione dei relativi adempimenti e verificandone la corretta attuazione da parte delle società in coerenza con quanto definito dalle procedure di Gruppo. Gestire i rapporti con ARERA, con tutti gli altri enti pubblici e tecnici preposti agli ambiti di competenza (in particolare Terna, Snam, GME, , ecc.), rappresentare la posizione aziendale con le associazioni di categoria nonché valutare gli impatti della regolazione sui risultati aziendali e supportare il business nello sviluppo delle nuove iniziative


**Per quale Società**

**A2A S.p.A.** In A2A l'organizzazione principale dei servizi di **Corporate** comprende le attività di guida, indirizzo strategico, coordinamento e controllo della gestione industriale, nonché i servizi a supporto del business e delle attività operative (esempio: servizi amministrativi e contabili, legali, di approvvigionamento, di gestione del personale, di information technology, di comunicazione servizi di fonia fissa e mobile, etc.) i cui costi, al netto di quanto riaddebitato per competenza alle singole Business Units in base ai servizi resi, rimangono in carico alla Corporate.


**Ti occuperai di**

* Presidiare all’interno del team la regolazione relativa al mercato elettrico e gas all’ingrosso, e quella dell’idrogeno nonché alle tematiche correlate (tra cui il market design, il capacity market, il MACSE e i sistemi di accumulo, i PPA ecc.), focalizzando le attività sugli ambiti a maggior valore aggiunto attuale o potenziale per il Gruppo;
* Partecipare al presidio dei rapporti operativi con gli stakeholder esterni, enti pubblici e tecnici predisposti alla regolazione dei settori di business in cui opera il Gruppo (ARERA, Terna, Snam, GME, ecc…) e rappresentazione degli interessi aziendali negli ambiti istituzionali e associativi, nazionali ed esteri;
* Partecipare alle analisi degli impatti della normativa in termini economici e sui processi aziendali, supportando le società nell’interpretazione ed implementazione della stessa allo scopo di cogliere occasioni di business correlate all’evoluzione del quadro di riferimento;
* Monitoraggio e analisi tecnico\-economica della regolamentazione e della normativa in discussione e di quella già adottata per valutarne gli impatti, le mitigazioni e le potenzialità per il Gruppo;
* Informative ed aggiornamento sui provvedimenti regolatori di interesse verso le strutture di business del Gruppo volta alla definizione della “posizione aziendale” e diffusione della stessa presso gli stakeholder interni;
* Partecipare alle riunioni delle associazioni di categoria di cui il Gruppo fa parte per rappresentare gli interessi aziendali in occasione dei processi consultivi promossi dagli enti regolatori nazionali ed internazionali;
* Applicazione delle policy e delle procedure interne al Gruppo A2A con riferimento all’ambito di regolazione di competenza per verificare l’adempimento operativo a cura delle società;
Supporto regolatorio nei gruppi di lavoro interfunzionali con particolare attenzione allo sviluppo delle nuove iniziative di business.  
* 

**Sei la persona giusta se ti riconosci in questo profilo**

* Laurea magistrale ad indirizzo tecnico o economico, con preferenza per laurea in ingegneria;
* Esperienza almeno biennale nel settore energetico maturata in utilities, società di consulenza, centri di ricerca, enti, istituzioni, con priorità ad esperienze nella regolazione;
* Conoscenza avanzata del pacchetto Office;
* Ottima conoscenza della lingua inglese;
Completano il profilo: orientamento al teamworking, flessibilità, concretezza e spirito collaborativo, problem solving, capacità di analisi e di pianificazione/organizzazione del lavoro, proattività e spirito di iniziativa.  
* 

**Che cosa offriamo**

* **Flessibilità:** settimana lavorativa con venerdì a orario ridotto, flessibilità negli orari di entrata/uscita e pausa pranzo; **60% di smartworking su base bimestrale**
* **Buoni pasto elettronici**
* **Salute e benessere** : assistenza sanitaria integrativa, supporto psicologico attraverso counseling, webinar dedicati al benessere psicofisico e vaccino antinfluenzale
* **Famiglia** : A2A Life Caring, iniziativa a supporto della genitorialità attraverso un mese aggiuntivo retribuito al 100% per maternità/paternità, rimborsi spese per figli, convenzioni con asili nido, team coaching per neogenitori; spazio caregiver, offriamo webinar e uno spazio di ascolto dedicati a chi si prende cura di persone fragili o non autosufficienti
* **A2A Life Sharing** : Piano di Azionariato Diffuso del Gruppo A2A che prevede l’assegnazione di azioni ordinarie di A2A con la possibilità di beneficiare di ulteriori azioni nel tempo
* **Formazione e sviluppo** : programma di induction dedicato ai nuovi assunti, gestione dei talenti, corsi di formazione e possibilità di crescita interna e job rotation tramite candidatura interna
* **Risparmio e tempo libero** : oltre 300 convenzioni, tra cui trasporti pubblici, attività sportive, viaggi, eventi culturali, e offerte speciali per luce e gas. Opzioni per il car sharing e la conversione del premio di risultato in benefit di welfare
* **Ambiente di lavoro** : promuoviamo una cultura che valorizza la diversità, l’equità e l'inclusione (DE\&I); potrai inoltre avere la possibilità di unirti a un team dedicato allo sviluppo di iniziative e progetti su queste tematiche. Sosteniamo l'evoluzione tecnologica e offriamo strumenti e risorse per promuovere il lavoro digitale.

*Saranno valutate esclusivamente le candidature inviate tramite il Sito Carriere aziendale.*

*Ci impegniamo ogni giorno per creare un ambiente di lavoro inclusivo e innovativo, fondato sul rispetto, sull’etica e sulle pari opportunità garantendo la valorizzazione delle diversità.*

*La selezione è rivolta a candidati e candidate in linea con le competenze richieste. I dati personali raccolti verranno trattati nel rispetto del Regolamento Ue 2016/679 (GDPR). Tutte le nostre opportunità sono aperte anche a profili tutelati da Legge 68/99\.*

title: Local Regulatory Affairs Manager (Area US) - Mendrisio site (Ticino)
Supplier & Co-Manufacturer Quality Manager EMEA
description:
Ubicazione: Sesto San Giovanni, ITA
Categoria: Supply Chain
Quality
Mid\-Senior level
Hybrid
  
**Descrizione**
---------------


Campari Group today is a major player in the global branded spirits industry, with a portfolio of over 50 premium and super premium brands, marketed and distributed in over 190 markets around the world, with leading positions in Europe and the Americas.

  



Headquartered in Milan, Italy, Campari Group owns 22 plants worldwide and has its own distribution network in 22 countries, and employs approximately 4,000 people.

  

Shares of the parent company Davide Campari \- Milano N.V. are listed on the Italian Stock Exchange since 2001\. Campari Group is today the sixth\-largest player worldwide in the premium spirits industry.

  

Based in the Campari Group Head Office, reporting to the EMEA Quality Manager , he/she will be responsible for the day to day management of suppliers and external manufactures for all ingredient, packaging and product cat\-egories. This involves the pro\-active management of the categories of suppliers, the development and implemen\-tation of quality and performance improvement programs, while supporting key initiatives of the activity involving new suppliers and new products.


The EMEA Supplier Quality Manager is responsible for the execution of the organization's Supplier Quality Pro\-gram, he/she will be a part of the Global Quality Suppliers and 3rd Party Manufacturer Committee . He/She will also be the business partner of EMEA factories and regional stakeholders, providing support and coordinating with the others supplier quality teams’ members as appropriate.

**Main Responsibilities**

* Vendor Management \- Responsible for the management of an evolving Global Vendor Management Strat\-egy to assure a continuous supply of safe, legal and specified materials.
* Leads supplier selection and supplier qualifications activities, ensuring suppliers / external operations meet Campari Group Vision quality and regulatory requirements.
* Manage performance and partners with suppliers and external manufacturers to address challenges and execute improvement plans.
* Effectively support key business initiatives such as cost reduction projects and new product development, ensuring quality from design and compliance right from the first deliveries.
* Vendor Auditing \- Audit and inspect vendors to assure their full compliance with Campari Group Quality re\-quirements and specifications.

**Primary Activities**

* Ingredient and Packaging Suppliers \- Provide raw material and packaging expertise which includes pertinent performance and data analyses that ultimately supports the proactive category plan as define by the Supplier Quality Assurance Program.
* Work with the manufacturing units to resolve issues related to raw materials and vendors.
* Lead mitigation of key supply chain issues/risks (vendors related) to improve and increase supplier reliability and performance.
* Conduct supplier qualification activities at existing and prospective supplier and/or external manufacturers as needed.
* Responsible for managing supplier related systems which include but not limited to change control, NC/CAPA, Quality Agreements, specification development.
* Provide guidance and support to project teams and affiliates regarding outsourced activities.
* Track and trend key quality indicators / metrics to monitor vendors performance.
* Responsible for quality technical assessments, quality agreements, supplier change control, supplier non\-conformance, and CAPA related activities.
* Partner with, Quality, R\&D, Engineering, Manufacturing, Purchasing, Distribution, Regulatory and vendors to ensure compliance to product requirements, material/component specifications, company procedures, FDA, ISO, GFSI etc.
* Supports RD in production trials of new product releases across the suppliers/co\-manufacturing facilities
* Rolls out of the new Vendors SAQ platform through the Emea Region.
* Perform other duties, as required or assigned by the Global SAQ team and Emea Quality Director


Performance Indicators

* Annual Goals aligned with the Company’s Must Win Battles and Management Review of Quality Process (RFT, Complaints Reduction, Defect reduction, Cost of Non Quality, Audits plan, Vendor Performance rating etc)
* Individual goals aligned with implementing and maintaining a Quality Culture

**Competence/Skill Requirements**

* Relevant food industry experience required with experience in supplier quality function (3 years minimum) and in raw materials or related areas.
* Strong communication, negotiation and influencing skills.
* GFSI Lead auditor certification
* Strong working knowledge of food processes, HACCP, risk management and sanitation ideally gained in a food manufacturing environment.
* Knowledge in food safety raw materials and quality systems programs throughout a range of industry areas.
* Experience in managing multi country vendors.
* Very good team player. Needs to be firm but have diplomacy and ability to influence behaviors.
* Good listening skills, auditing skills, analytical skills good initiative and autonomy.
* Fluent in English, other European languages highly appreciated.

**Knowledge and Experience**

* A Bachelor degree in Food Science or equivalent Applied Science.
* Advanced Knowledge of the Beverage and Spirits Product Category
* Extensive experience in Quality Assurance preferably with practical experience as Food safety expert, R\&D or manufacturing function
* Advanced knowledge of HACCP and microbial potential hazards and GFSI standards
* Experience in quality systems and regulated businesses for Food \& Beverages
* Good understanding of customer and consumer centric quality management principles
* Knowledge and application of base QA systems, including deviations management, consumer complaints, document control and technical standards within a regulated knowledge of microbiology and analytical labs methods, DoE (Design of Experiments), PDCA, Six\-Sigma and/or Lean Manufacturing tools
* Proven track record in risk assessment and management, including risk identification tools and communication processes
* Solid Experience with manufacturing operations
* Project management skills –being able to lead multiple projects independently in a fast\-paced environment
* Experience in working across multiple functions/projects
* Broad understanding of Supply Chain models
* Fluent both in italian and english

 **Our commitment to Diversity \& Inclusion:**


At Campari Group we believe in building more value together, thus we see diversity in all forms as a source of enrichment. Our employment policies and practices ensure that we are committed to providing equal employment opportunities in all aspects of employment without regard to any individual’s race, religion, creed, color, national origin, ancestry, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, marital status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or characteristics or expression, political affiliation or activity, age, veteran status, citizenship, or any other characteristic protected by law.

 **Note to applicants:**


Your application will be assessed based on your abilities, expertise, general knowledge and experience, not because of any confidential, proprietary or trade secret information you may possess. You must not disclose to Campari Group any such information. In the event that you are asked a question that cannot be answered without disclosure of any confidential, proprietary or trade secret information (including from a current or prior employer or their vendors or customers), you must decline to answer the question.

  



**Notice to third party agencies:**


Please refrain from cold\-calling or emailing our executive leadership team or the HR community directly. The Talent Acquisition department manages centralized recruiting operations globally, including the selection and management of external suppliers. Currently, our preferred supplier list is at full capacity. To ensure we have your information on file for future consideration, we kindly request that you complete the online form provided here.

title: Manager, Energy & Infrastructure Advisory
description:
JLL supports the Whole You, personally and professionally.
  
  

Our people at JLL are shaping the future of real estate for a better world by combining world class services, advisory and technology to our clients. We are committed to hiring the best, most talented people in our industry; and we support them through professional growth, flexibility, and personalized benefits to manage life in and outside of work. Whether you’ve got deep experience in commercial real estate, skilled trades, and technology, or you’re looking to apply your relevant experience to a new industry, we empower you to shape a brighter way forward so you can thrive professionally and personally.
  
  

Supporting the management of energy and infrastructure transactions under the guidance of Associate Directors and Directors, working closely with the broader Italian team and supervising analysts.
  
  

**Key Responsibilities:**  

Advising clients on transaction matters with appropriate oversight and support, ensuring clear communication and alignment with client objectives.
  
Developing and reviewing financial models and contributing to the preparation of transaction marketing materials and commercial advisory reports that support informed decision\-making.
  
Supporting business development activities and assisting in the generation of client leads through proactive market engagement.
  
Participating in industry events and client meetings, representing the firm's expertise and building relationships.
  
Contributing to thought leadership articles and market commentary on energy topics, sharing insights that reflect current market dynamics.
  
Mentoring and managing junior team members on day\-to\-day assignments, fostering their professional development.
  
Supporting team development initiatives and contributing to the growth of the practice through knowledge sharing and collaboration.
  
Taking increased responsibility for client relationships and transaction execution as experience develops, demonstrating growing autonomy and strategic thinking.
  
  

**Required Skills and Experience:**  

Hold a recognised financial professional qualification and/or an MBA, with a minimum of 4–5 years' experience within the energy sector, energy transition, or infrastructure advisory environment.
  
Demonstrable experience in a financial, commercial, or advisory role, ideally related to infrastructure, energy, or regulated asset projects.
  
  

**Full proficiency in financial modelling, including:**  

Building integrated three\-statement financial models (P\&L, Cash Flow, Balance Sheet) from scratch, with clear logic, transparency, and auditability.
  
  

Developing and reviewing project finance, corporate finance, and valuation models (e.g. DCF, IRR, NPV, multiples where relevant).
  
  

Preparing and analysing sensitivities, scenarios, and data tables to assess key value drivers and risks.
  
  

Performing valuation impact analysis of commercial offers, contractual terms, financing structures, and agreements (e.g. offtake contracts, concessions, PPAs, regulatory changes).
  
  

Ensuring models are suitable for investment decision\-making, lender review, and client presentation.
  
  

Strong capability in quantitative and qualitative research focused on the energy and infrastructure sectors, including market fundamentals, regulatory frameworks, technology trends, cost drivers, and competitive dynamics.
  
Experience producing high\-quality written materials for clients and investors, such as investment memoranda, financial packs, presentations, and supporting analyses.
  
Proven ability to interact directly with clients, manage stakeholders, and lead projects or discrete workstreams, taking ownership of deliverables and timelines.
  
Ability to work effectively under pressure, manage multiple priorities, and deliver high\-quality outputs within tight and often changing deadlines.
  
Willingness to travel for client meetings as required.
  
Fluency (verbal and written) in Italian and English. Additional languages are welcomed and valued.
  
A collaborative and open approach, with strong team orientation, intellectual curiosity, and a commitment to continuous learning and professional development. We value diverse perspectives and encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply.
  
  

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
  
  

Descrizione del Ruolo
  
Supporto alla gestione di operazioni nel settore energetico e infrastrutturale sotto la guida di Associate Director e Director, collaborando strettamente con il team italiano e supervisionando gli analisti:
  
  

**Responsabilità Principali:**  

Consulenza ai clienti su operazioni e transazioni con adeguata supervisione e supporto, garantendo una comunicazione chiara e l'allineamento con gli obiettivi del cliente.
  
Sviluppo e revisione di modelli finanziari e contributo alla preparazione di materiali di marketing per transazioni e report di consulenza commerciale a supporto di decisioni informate.
  
Supporto alle attività di sviluppo del business e collaborazione nella generazione di nuove opportunità commerciali attraverso un coinvolgimento proattivo del mercato.
  
Partecipazione a eventi di settore e incontri con i clienti, rappresentando l'expertise della società e costruendo relazioni strategiche.
  
Contributo alla redazione di articoli di thought leadership e commenti di mercato su tematiche energetiche, condividendo analisi che riflettono le dinamiche attuali del mercato.
  
Mentoring e gestione di membri junior del team nelle attività quotidiane, favorendone lo sviluppo professionale.
  
Supporto alle iniziative di sviluppo del team e contributo alla crescita della practice attraverso la condivisione di conoscenze e la collaborazione.
  
Assunzione progressiva di maggiori responsabilità nella gestione dei rapporti con i clienti e nell'esecuzione delle transazioni, dimostrando crescente autonomia e capacità di pensiero strategico.
  
  

Competenze ed Esperienza Richieste
  
Possesso di una qualifica professionale finanziaria riconosciuta e/o MBA, con un minimo di 4–5 anni di esperienza nel settore energetico, nella transizione energetica o nell'advisory di infrastrutture.
  
Esperienza comprovata in un ruolo finanziario, commerciale o di consulenza, idealmente legato a progetti infrastrutturali, energetici o di asset regolamentati.
  
  

**Piena competenza nella modellistica finanziaria, tra cui:**  

Costruzione da zero di modelli finanziari integrati a tre prospetti (Conto Economico, Rendiconto Finanziario, Stato Patrimoniale), con logica chiara, trasparenza e tracciabilità.
  
  

Sviluppo e revisione di modelli di project finance, corporate finance e valutazione (es. DCF, IRR, NPV, multipli ove pertinenti).
  
  

Preparazione e analisi di sensibilità, scenari e tabelle dati per valutare i principali driver di valore e rischi.
  
  

Analisi dell'impatto valutativo di offerte commerciali, termini contrattuali, strutture di finanziamento e accordi (es. contratti di offtake, concessioni, PPA, modifiche normative).
  
  

Garanzia che i modelli siano idonei per decisioni di investimento, revisione da parte di finanziatori e presentazione ai clienti.
  
  

Forte capacità di ricerca quantitativa e qualitativa focalizzata sui settori energetico e infrastrutturale, includendo fondamentali di mercato, contesti normativi, trend tecnologici, driver di costo e dinamiche competitive.
  
Esperienza nella produzione di materiali scritti di alta qualità per clienti e investitori, quali memorandum di investimento, pacchetti finanziari, presentazioni e analisi di supporto.
  
Comprovata capacità di interagire direttamente con i clienti, gestire stakeholder e guidare progetti o specifiche aree di lavoro, assumendosi la responsabilità di deliverable e tempistiche.
  
Capacità di lavorare efficacemente sotto pressione, gestire priorità multiple e produrre output di alta qualità entro scadenze serrate e spesso mutevoli.
  
Disponibilità a viaggiare per incontri con clienti secondo necessità.
  
Padronanza (parlata e scritta) dell'italiano e dell'inglese. Ulteriori lingue sono gradite e valorizzate.
  
Un approccio collaborativo e aperto, con forte orientamento al team, curiosità intellettuale e impegno verso l'apprendimento continuo e lo sviluppo professionale. Valorizziamo prospettive diverse e incoraggiamo candidature da persone di ogni background.
  
If this job description resonates with you, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet all of the requirements below. We’re interested in getting to know you and what you bring to the table!
  
  

Personalized benefits that support personal well\-being and growth:
  
  

JLL recognizes the impact that the workplace can have on your wellness, so we offer a supportive culture and comprehensive benefits package that prioritizes mental, physical and emotional health.
  
  

About JLL –
  
  

We’re JLL—a leading professional services and investment management firm specializing in real estate. We have operations in over 80 countries and a workforce of over 102,000 individuals around the world who help real estate owners, occupiers and investors achieve their business ambitions. As a global Fortune 500 company, we also have an inherent responsibility to drive sustainability and corporate social responsibility. That’s why we’re committed to our purpose to shape the future of real estate for a better world. We’re using the most advanced technology to create rewarding opportunities, amazing spaces and sustainable real estate solutions for our clients, our people, and our communities.
  
  

Our core values of teamwork, ethics and excellence are also fundamental to everything we do and we’re honored to be recognized with awards for our success by organizations both globally and locally.
  
  

Creating a diverse and inclusive culture where we all feel welcomed, valued and empowered to achieve our full potential is important to who we are today and where we’re headed in the future. And we know that unique backgrounds, experiences and perspectives help us think bigger, spark innovation and succeed together.

title: Auto Finance Manager
description:
#### **About BYD**


BYD is one of the world’s leading manufacturers of new energy vehicles (NEVs), dedicated to driving sustainable mobility solutions across global markets. In Europe, BYD is expanding rapidly and building innovative financing and leasing solutions to support our customers, dealers, and strategic partners.

#### **Position Summary**


We are seeking a motivated and detail\-oriented Auto Finance Manager to support BYD’s finance and leasing activities in a specific European market. This role focuses on developing, coordinating, and implementing local retail, captive leasing and any other finance solutions in close cooperation with local internal teams and external partners (banks, leasing companies, and dealers, etc. ). The position requires strong financial acumen, experience in auto finance/leasing, and excellent stakeholder management skills to support BYD’s business growth in the local market.

#### **Key Responsibilities:**

**Auto Finance / Captive Lease**

* Work with local finance, sales, aftersales and legal teams to develop and roll out finance programs.
* Design and manage retail and business finance products (leasing, loans, balloon, etc.) to support BYD’s sales targets.
* Partner with banks and leasing companies to optimise offers, pricing and customer experience.
* Track portfolio performance (penetration, margin, risk) and report insights to management.Wholesale \& Dealer Financing
* Establish and manage relationships with local banks, lenders, and other financial partners to secure competitive funding lines.
* Negotiate wholesale credit facilities to support BYD’s dealer network development and expansion in the country.
* Optimise floorplan (inventory) financing structures to align dealer stock levels with local market demand.
* Conduct financial health assessments for franchise/dealer partners and provide risk\-based recommendations.
* Strategy, Market Intelligence \& Compliance
* Monitor and assess local market trends, competitor activities, and regulatory developments in auto finance and leasing to support strategic decision\-making.
* Ensure regulatory and internal policy compliance across all local financing and leasing activities.
* Coordinate with BYD Group finance and legal teams on cross\-border and group\-level financing agreements that impact the local market.
* Prepare and present strategic reports, business cases, and improvement suggestions to senior management.
* Stakeholder Management \& Training
* Act as a liaison between BYD Europe HQ and the local country teams, ensuring consistent implementation of leasing and finance policies, products, and frameworks.
* Deliver training to local sales and dealer staff on finance/leasing product features, processes, and digital sales tools.
* Travel within the country (and occasionally to other BYD locations) to meet with leasing partners, banks, dealers, and internal stakeholders.
* Undertake additional responsibilities and tasks as assigned by the Finance Controller or senior management.

#### **Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree or above in Finance, Business, Accounting, or related fields.
* Minimum 5 years’ experience in auto finance, preferably within captive finance, leasing companies or banks.
* Strong analytical, numerical, and problem\-solving skills, with experience in financial analysis and portfolio monitoring.
* Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to build strong relationships with external partners and internal stakeholders.
* Proficiency in English is required; additional European languages (e.g. French, German, Dutch) are an advantage.
* Strong understanding of regulatory and compliance requirements in auto finance/leasing in the local market.
* Ability to work independently in a fast\-paced, international environment, and to manage multiple projects.

#### **What we offer:**

* Competitive compensation based on experience and performance.
* Health insurance (Metasalute) and meal vouchers.
* Company\-wide and department teambuilding events.
* The opportunity to contribute to Europe’s transition to zero\-emission transportation and the decarbonization of the economy.

#### **Our Purpose**


We are building a zero\-emission future that reconnects humanity with nature and a world of clean air. Join a diverse and dynamic team that strives to make a positive impact on the environment and society.

title: BESS/Grid Project Manager – Milan
description:
**Type of contract:** Permanent
  

**Location:** Milan, Italy (on site with business trips)  

**Start date:** as soon as possible

### **About NW**


NW aims to make the energy transition accessible to all. Since 2007, the group has been deploying innovative solutions to increase the share of low\-carbon energies in the French electricity mix, support the stability of the electricity network and contributing to the development of electric mobility.


As the first French energy transition unicorn, NW is the leader in France in electricity storage with its JBox solution and the precursor of high\-power charging terminals, its IECharge. The company is also expanding internationally, particularly in Europe and the United States. In February 2023, NW joined the FrenchTech Next40\.


Due to our remarkable growth, we are looking for a BESS/Grid Project Manager to come and collaborate with our team in Italy. Responsible for developing energy storage projects, you will have the following responsibilities:

### **What you will do**

* Manage the grid connection process (MV/HV) in collaboration with grid operators (e.g., e\-distribuzione, Terna, others).
* Coordinate the development of BESS projects in Italy, from permitting to execution.
* Monitor project timelines, costs, and regulatory compliance throughout the project lifecycle (PMO activities).
* Manage the grid connection process (LV/MV/HV) in collaboration with grid operators (e.g., e\-distribuzione, Terna, others).
* Support the preliminary and executive design phases.
* Collaborate with internal and external technical, legal, and environmental teams.
* Conduct market analysis and support the valorization of BESS solutions.

### **Desired skills**

* Knowledge of the electricity market in Italy is a plus, including interaction with Terna and E\-distribuzione
* Experience with battery energy storage systems (BESS)
* Proficiency in English (French is a plus)
* You have good analytical and multitasking skills
* You have excellent interpersonal and negotiation skills

### **Your profile**

* Degree in Engineering (Electrical, Energy, Civil, or similar) or equivalent technical background.
* Proven experience (minimum 2 years) in project management within the energy sector, preferably in BESS, solar PV, or wind.
* Technical knowledge in the field of renewable energy infrastructures and BESS
* Experience with grid connection procedures and key stakeholders (e.g., e\-distribuzione, Terna, others).
* Familiarity with permitting processes and national environmental regulations.
* Ability to work independently, manage suppliers, and coordinate multidisciplinary teams.
* Willingness to travel within Italy.

### **Why NW?**

* Join a dynamic and positive team conscious about our current climate challenges
* Discover the energy stocking and electric mobility industries
* Participate in company events
* Contribute to one of the most important transitions of our time aiming to achieve environmental stability for future generations

### **Recruitment process**

* HR interview
* Manager interview
* Final interview

title: Project Development Manager
description:
**Do you want to build a better world?**

**About Enfinity Global**


Enfinity Global is a purpose\-driven company focused on making a positive impact on the planet by helping companies, governments, and individuals transition to a carbon\-free and sustainable economy. Our role as a leading IPP is to develop, finance, build, operate, and own decarbonization assets in the long term in Europe, Asia, and the Americas through our offices in the USA, Spain, Italy, UK, Netherlands, India, and Japan.


Our team of over 400 Enfiniters comprises seasoned finance professionals, as well as experienced project developers and operators with extensive industry experience across all stages of the project life cycle. We pride ourselves on being creative and innovative solution providers to our customers and partners.

**Job Description:**


Enfinity Global is looking for a Project Development Manager for Italy market that will be responsible for developing and authorize the renewable projects in Italy, mapping and approaching with relevant stakeholders including grid companies, negotiating and concluding land agreements with landowners, advisors and consultants to follow the relevant permits applications, liaising with public agencies (municipalities, provinces and regions) to coordinate the filing and the development to the permitting process also in coordination with other departments such as legal and engineering, organizing and analyzing data rooms, tracking projects status, reporting to management being able to evaluate risks and provide advice and mitigation plan development, etc., on time, within budget, and in compliance with all technical, commercial, safety regulatory, and environmental requirements to meet or exceed company’s expectations.


The candidate will work within the development department working with the operation team. This opportunity has a strong project management component and candidates will be managing several projects simultaneously in cooperation with other members of the teams based in Spain, Portugal, and Italy. The candidate should also be able to resolve conflicts, communicate effectively with clients, be comfortable working in a small group environment, and willing to put in extra effort to coordinate activities across global time zones.

 **Requirements**  

* Support in Permitting Process: Assist Project Development Managers in managing the full permitting process for PV and BESS projects. This includes gathering necessary documentation, preparing permit applications, and coordinating with local authorities, municipalities, provinces, and regional agencies.
* Stakeholder Engagement: Help in identifying and engaging with external stakeholders such as landowners, grid operators, and public agencies. Assist in setting up meetings, preparing materials, and maintaining ongoing communication to support project development.
* Land Agreement Support: Assist in preparing documents and supporting negotiations for land agreements with property owners. Work with the legal team to ensure that contracts and agreements comply with regulatory and legal standards.
* Internal Coordination: Work with various internal teams, including engineering, legal, finance, and construction, to gather information and align project objectives. Assist in ensuring all project activities are well\-coordinated and meet the permitting requirements.
* Document Preparation \& Management: Support the preparation of project\-related documents, including permit applications, land agreements, project reports, and regulatory submissions. Ensure that all documentation is accurate, up to date, and filed appropriately.
* Research \& Analysis: Conduct market research and analysis on regulatory frameworks, environmental standards, and other factors affecting renewable energy development in Italy. Help identify potential risks and opportunities related to project development.

  

  

**Qualifications and Experience**

* Graduate in a relevant field such as renewable energy, engineering, environmental science, or business.
* \+3 years of experience in similar role
* A strong interest in the renewable energy sector, particularly in PV and BESS project development.
* Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment and manage multiple tasks simultaneously.
* Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.
* Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with both internal teams and external stakeholders.
* Proficiency in Italian and English is required.
* Knowledge of Italian regulatory frameworks for renewable energy is a plus, though not required.

**Personal Attributes**

* Enthusiastic, motivated, and eager to learn about the renewable energy industry.
* Strong problem\-solving skills with the ability to adapt to changing project needs.
* A proactive team player who is willing to take initiative and support the team in various project\-related tasks.
* Willingness to travel occasionally within Italy, if required.

  

**Benefits**  

* Private Health Insurance
* Pension Plan
* Paid Time Off
* Work From Home
* Training \& Development
* Performance Bonus

**Discover your future at Citi**

Working at Citi is far more than just a job. A career with us means joining a team of more than 230,000 dedicated people from around the globe. At Citi, you’ll have the opportunity to grow your career, give back to your community and make a real impact.


**Job Overview**
----------------


The **Commercial Subsidiary Group (CSG)** focuses on providing banking solutions to the subsidiaries of multinational corporations. These subsidiaries often operate across diverse markets, and CSG supports them with tailored financial products, risk management, liquidity solutions, and credit services. The group acts as the strategic link between the global parent company and its local operating entities, ensuring consistency, governance, and seamless execution across countries.


The **CSG banker** would be responsible for managing relationships with subsidiary clients, understanding their operational needs, and delivering end\-to\-end financial solutions. This includes advising on cash management, working capital, credit, FX, trade finance, and digital banking tools. The banker collaborates closely with product partners and the global parent coverage team to ensure the subsidiary’s goals align with the broader corporate strategy. They act as a trusted advisor, helping clients navigate local markets while maintaining the connectivity and standards expected by global organizations.

title: Trust & safety manager
description:
At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards.  

  

In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other.  

  

For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate.


A few examples of your responsibilities

* Enhance user safety. Monitor emerging threats, abuse patterns, and relevant regulatory developments at scale—ensuring they are effectively reflected in products used by millions.
* Implement our Trust \& Safety framework. Adapt general best practices to product\-specific risks and needs, collaborating with Product and Engineering teams to embed safety into features and workflows.
* Cooperate with moderation teams. Ensure clarity on evolving policies and streamline moderation processes, personally handling critical or sensitive escalations whenever necessary.


What we look for

* Reasoning ability. Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter.
* Drive. You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar.
* Team spirit. You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent.
* Proficiency in English. You read, write, and speak proficiently in English.


What we offer

* Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams. You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field.
* An exceptional opportunity for growth. We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too.
* Competitive pay and access to equity in the company. Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £41,255 in London and €39,892 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £56,345 and £85,797 in London, and €54,346 and €82,557 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut.
* All. These. Benefits. Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work.


The selection process


In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject.
If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours.
We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times.
We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form.


Before you apply


If you’ve applied before but didn’t receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again.


Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment.


To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we expect you to spend most days in our Milan office during your first few months with us, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from one of our offices, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage.


If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

title: R&D Manager
description:
StarTric is a fast\-paced cardiovascular startup developing **TriClover**, an innovative Class III transcatheter repair device to treat tricuspid regurgitation. We’re seeking a **hands\-on Medical Device Specialist** to join our growing team. This hybrid role spans **project management, R\&D, verification \& validation, industrialization, and regulatory/quality interface**, with a direct reporting line to the CEO and close collaboration with the CTO and COO.

The ideal candidate will take a central role in the end\-to\-end development of a complex implantable device, progressively taking ownership of the full product lifecycle and contributing to establishing StarTric as an integrated, full\-cycle medical device manufacturer.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Lead and coordinate day\-to\-day development activities of a Class III implantable device.
* Manage project timelines, technical milestones, and cross\-functional partners (clinical, R\&D, regulatory, manufacturing).
* Drive the design control process: inputs/outputs, materials, architecture, risk management, and reviews.
* Oversee V\&V strategy and execution, including preclinical testing and traceability documentation.
* Act as the main liaison with quality and regulatory partners, ensuring MDR/ISO 13485/14971 compliance.
* Support industrialization: manufacturability, supplier qualification, process transfer, and scale\-up.
* Maintain design history and technical documentation (DHFs, protocols, V\&V records).
* Mentor junior team members and promote best practices across documentation and development.

**Profile**

* 3–7 years’ experience in medical device development (preferably Class III / cardiovascular / structural heart).
* Background in Biomedical, Mechanical Engineering, or Materials Science; advanced degree preferred.
* Deep understanding of design control, V\&V methods, preclinical testing, and system\-level development.
* Familiar with EU MDR, ISO 13485, ISO 14971; regulatory submission experience is a plus.
* Strong project management, documentation, and cross\-functional collaboration skills.
* Fluent in English; Italian proficiency highly desirable.

**What We Offer**

* A key role in a **mission\-driven startup** advancing a **next\-generation therapy for TR**.
* High ownership, direct impact, and close collaboration with world\-leading experts in structural heart.
* Competitive compensation and flexible/hybrid working arrangements.

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: €30,000\.00 \- €60,000\.00 per year

Application Question(s):

* 3–7 years’ experience in medical device development?

Structural heart experience?

title: SDS Compliance Specialist
description:
**SDS Compliance Specialist**

**Reports to:** Global Logistics \& Trade Compliance Manager

**Location:** Yuma

**Role Purpose:**


The SDS and Logistics Compliance Specialist is responsible for ensuring global regulatory compliance for safety data sheets (SDS) and product freight labeling and owning logistics\-related master data and processes. This role combines SDS creation, maintenance, and training with logistics classification, cost management, and continuous improvement initiatives. Evaluates the effectiveness of safety and security programs. Reviews current safety training and recommends revisions, improvements, and updates.

**Essential Duties and Responsibilities:**

**SDS Safety Management:**


1\. Oversee global SDS creation and maintenance for all commercial and development products across regions


2\. Ensure regulatory compliance with SDS, GHS, and country\-specific requirements


3\. Guide country\-specific regulations and requirements


4\. Maintain and Input product toxicity information data in the ERP system (E1\) for all skus


5\. Maintain current international and country\-specific certifications


6\. Train employees on SDS, GHS, and country regulations


7\. Conduct compliance audits of branch locations and drive corrective actions


8\. Serve as the main contact for Chemtrec emergency response services for the U.S and Canada

**Labeling:**


1\. Review global product transportation labels for compliance with SDS, GHS, and registration requirements


2\. Approve packaging artwork to ensure regulatory transportation compliance


Logistics Classification \& Master Data:


1\. Classify products for proper transportation modes and regulations


2\. Enter transport classification and Limited Quantity exceptions in the ERP system


3\. Keep current on industry logistics practices and benchmarks


4\. Understand and analyze logistics costs and performance, identifying improvement opportunities


5\. Oversee the aggregate logistics budget and provide feedback to local markets


6\. Manage and rationalize logistics\-related systems and data


7\. Advise supply chain departments (logistics, purchasing, customer service) on disputing incorrect freight charges


due to misclassification by third\-party logistics companies.

**Skills and Qualifications:**


 Project management skills


 Ability to work in multicultural and multidiscipline teams


 Fluency in English and Spanish

**Required Qualifications:**


 Bachelor's degree in supply chain, logistics, chemistry, or a related field

title: Supplier Quality Management and Audit Category Specialist II Ingredients
description:
Cargill is committed to providing food and agricultural solutions to nourish the world in a safe, responsible, and sustainable way. Sitting at the heart of the supply chain, we partner with farmers and customers to source, make and deliver products that are vital for living.


Our 155,000 team members innovate with purpose, providing customers with life’s essentials so businesses can grow, communities prosper, and consumers live well. With over 160 years of experience as a family company, we look ahead while remaining true to our values. We put people first. We reach higher. We do the right thing—today and for generations to come.

**Job Purpose and Impact**
--------------------------



The Supplier Quality Management and Audit Category Specialist will perform product safety risk management activities for materials, suppliers, external manufacturing facilities and other services relevant to the safe production of products. In this role, you will collaborate with multiple departments to ensure vendor evaluations are properly completed to identify risk and appropriate due diligence is given to mitigate risks related to vendors and materials. You will partner with vendors to ensure materials, supplier sources and external manufacturer's products comply with minimum product safety, quality and regulatory standards and assess preventative controls to ensure material and supplier risk evaluations meets requirements. This role is EU‑based. Candidates located in any EU country where Cargill operates are eligible for consideration. The position can be performed in a hybrid or fully remote setup, depending on local policy. This is a temporary position with a duration of 10 months.

**Key Accountabilities**
------------------------


* Perform ongoing relationship management activities, such as meeting key performance indicators, building a supplier scorecard, trending claims and complaints and material, supplier and external manufacture maintenance activities on required documentation and information.
* Investigate supplier incidents, gather data to handle and resolve standard incidents and complaints to provide support in one or more domain areas of knowledge and conduct root cause analysis on key incidents.
* Audit suppliers, external manufacturers, internal product safety audits and company facility evaluations within a defined category and coordinate efforts with the locations to ensure corrective actions or follow up on audit findings are completed within the required timeframe and meet requirements.
* Coordinate supplier and external manufacturer and material evaluation process to ensure the use of approved vendors who have met the company's requirements and have completed the required product safety improvements.
* Support new location and material setup along with completed supplier and material qualifications in the defined supplier database.
* Develop and deliver product supplier quality management training to assist in coaching, mentoring and developing new team members or key stakeholders and generate key information for troubleshooting and stakeholder knowledge sharing and training.
* Independently solve moderately complex issues with minimal supervision, while escalating more complex issues to appropriate staff.
* Other duties as assigned
**Qualifications**
------------------


### **Minimum Qualifications**

* Bachelor's degree in a related field or equivalent experience
* Experience in product safety, quality and auditing systems
* Minimum requirement of 2 years of relevant work experience.

### **Preferred Qualifications**

* Knowledge of product safety management systems with the ability to objectively evaluate product safety systems and report findings in an organized manner
* Knowledge establishing material and vendor risk and ensuring appropriate mitigation is defined
* Company qualified auditor with experience conducting product safety audits
**Our Offer**  

We provide a fast paced stimulating international environment, which will stretch your abilities and channel your talents. We also offer competitive salary and benefits combined with outstanding career development opportunities in one of the largest and most solid private companies in the world.


We welcome applications from people with disabilities and are committed to providing an inclusive, accessible recruitment process and workplace. We encourage candidates to let us know if they require any accommodations during the recruitment or interview process so we can support them appropriately.

 **Interested? Then make sure to send us your CV and cover letter in English today:**  

**Follow us on LinkedIn:** **https://www.linkedin.com/company/cargill**


Cargill is committed to being an inclusive employer. Click here to find out more https://careers.cargill.com/diversity/

**Background \& Experience:*** Experience with working within multi\-national businesses
* Experience working within a company that has Business/Shared services where accounting activities are performed centrally


Element offers you the chance to be part of an enthusiastic and motivated team, to work within an international organization with numerous development opportunities. It offers you a full\-time job with flexible working hours.  

If you think you are the right person to fill this role go to Element.com and submit your C.V.
Company Overview:

Element è una delle aziende in più rapida crescita al mondo nel settore del testing, ispezione e certificazione. A livello globale abbiamo più di 9\.000 menti brillanti che operano in 270 siti in 30 paesi. Insieme condividiamo uno scopo ambizioso: "Rendere il domani più sicuro dell'oggi".  

Quando il fallimento non è un'opzione, aiutiamo i clienti a fare in modo che i loro prodotti, materiali, processi e servizi siano sicuri, conformi e adatti allo scopo. Dalle prime fasi di R\&S, attraverso complesse approvazioni normative, fino alla produzione, la nostra rete globale di laboratori di scienziati, ingegneri e tecnici aiuta i clienti a ottenere la garanzia della qualità del prodotto.  

Anche se siamo orgogliosi della nostra dimensione a livello mondiale, lavorare alla Element è come far parte di un'azienda più piccola. Ti diamo la possibilità di prendere in mano la tua carriera, premiamo l'eccellenza e l'integrità con la crescita e lo sviluppo.  

Le industrie di tutto il mondo dipendono dalla nostra cura, dall'attenzione ai dettagli e dalla precisione assoluta del nostro lavoro. Il ruolo che dobbiamo svolgere nella creazione di un mondo più sicuro è molto più grande della nostra organizzazione.
Diversity Statement:

In Element siamo sempre orgogliosi di mettere al primo posto le nostre persone. Siamo un datore di lavoro con pari opportunità che riconosce la diversità e l'inclusione come fondamentali per la nostra visione di diventare "il partner di test più affidabile al mondo".  

Tutti i candidati adeguatamente qualificati saranno presi in considerazione per l'assunzione sulla base di criteri oggettivi legati al lavoro e senza tener conto di età, disabilità, origine etnica, sesso, stato civile, razza, religione, responsabilità di persone a carico, orientamento sessuale o identità di genere o altre caratteristiche in conformità con le leggi vigenti.

title: Demand Generation Advisory (BTP, BDC & AI) - Italy
description:
**We help the world run better**  

At SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed. **What you'll do**:  

As a Demand Generation Advisor, you will oversee Demand Generation programs for **Business Technology Platform (BTP), Business Data Cloud (BDC), and AI** across the EMEA region, acting as the **SPOC for Italy** within the team.  

You will support complex deals from both a sales and consumption perspective and collaborate with a wide network of stakeholders, including the Sales Director, Account Executives (BTP and Industry), CBO and local management, Demand Management, Marketing, Partners/Channel, Digital Hub, and CSPs.  

You will be the go‑to person responsible for tracking, coordinating, and initiating programmatic activities designed to:  

* Grow pipeline through deal clinics, events, campaigns, account‑based activities, partner engagements, and customer discovery workshops
* Support complex sales cycles, including commercial discussions and deal progression

  

You will act as the primary point of reference for the sales team on product and solution questions, leveraging your expertise to support the full sales cycle—from opportunity identification to post‑sale customer engagement and adoption.  

In addition, you will help customers expand their SAP footprint through:  

* Whitespace campaigns and account opportunity analysis
* Delivering solution presentations and participating in events to identify and qualify new opportunities
* Evaluating account qualification criteria
* Establishing yourself as a thought leader within account teams
* Transitioning engagements to implementation partners and Services
* Continuing to engage customers to reinforce SAP’s solution vision
* Leveraging digital assets throughout the customer journey

 **What you bring:**  

* 10\+ years of professional experience with large software/IT organizations, preferably in a solution\-specific segment
* Ability to build programs and track progress with multiple stakeholders.
* SAP product experience in BTP, BDC and AI highly advantageous.
* SAP Commercial / Sales experience is highly advantageous.
* Focus on creating compelling storylines and themes for customer interactions
* Ability to quickly understand customer business goals and ensure they are at the center of recommended solutions

 **Bring out your best**  

SAP innovations help more than four hundred thousand customers worldwide work together more efficiently and use business insight more effectively. Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to become a market leader in end\-to\-end business application software and related services for database, analytics, intelligent technologies, and experience management. At SAP, we believe we are made stronger by the unique capabilities and qualities that each person brings to our company, and we invest in our employees to inspire confidence and help everyone realize their full potential. We ultimately believe in unleashing all talent and creating a better world.
